schema.name: TZPID source truth registry gap fill
schema.basis: folder-local candidate promotion with safety gate

theory.canonical_statement: Every theory of fundamental physics eventually faces the engineering question: can it be built? The Trawin Zero Point framework, with its account of vacuum energy extraction and field driven rotation, naturally suggests a propulsion application — a drive that produces thrust not by expelling reaction mass but by coupling to the structure of spacetime itself. The corpus names this the Vimana drive, after the flying machines of ancient Sanskrit texts — not as a claim about ancient technology, but as a name for the class of propulsion that the TZP framework makes conceivable: a toroidal gyromagnetic device that negates gravity directionally and produces thrust through the unified propulsion field.
theory.technical_interpretation: ts expel air; propellers push water. The thrust is always paired with an equal and opposite expulsion of reaction mass. The unified propulsion field (ID 216) proposes a different principle. The emergent thrust axis of the Inward-Outward Cross chapter — the vector $\mathbf{v}_\text{thrust} \propto \mathbf{v}_\text{in}\times\mathbf{v}_\text{out}$ born from the orthogonal collision of inward and outward field flows — is a directed force that does not require expelled reaction mass. Instead, the reaction is against the structure of the vacuum field itself. The propulsion field is defined by: The first term is the gradient of the magnetic dipole force; the second is the gyromagnetic coupling to the TZP field. The net force is directed along the emergent thrust axis — the direction orthogonal to both the inward (magnetic) and outward (rotational) flows. **The thrust reacts against the vacuum, In technical terms, the entry packages the selected formula as the operative carrier, the nearby equations as the progression layer, and the symbol key as the compact interpretation of variables used by the source.

dictionary_sections.definition: The Propulsion Field is a source-backed Opera Omnia registry packet centered on the canonical relation \mathbf{F}_\text{prop} = \nabla\times(\boldsymbol{\mu}\times\mathbf{B}) + \lambda_g\,\mathbf{L}\times\mathbf{B}_\text{TZP}. It identifies the mathematical spine of the cited chapter and gives the entry a concrete equation, symbol key, and derivation chain for the four-page production layout.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: Every theory of fundamental physics eventually faces the engineering question: can it be built? The Trawin Zero Point framework, with its account of vacuum energy extraction and field driven rotation, naturally suggests a propulsion application — a drive that produces thrust not by expelling reaction mass but by coupling to the structure of spacetime itself. The corpus names this the Vimana drive, after the flying machines of ancient Sanskrit texts — not as a claim about ancient technology, but as a name for the class of propulsion that the TZP framework makes conceivable: a toroidal gyromagnetic device that negates gravity directionally and produces thrust through the unified propulsion field. The entry should be read as part of the larger Opera Omnia progression: a local equation is selected, its surrounding prose supplies the physical or mathematical role, and the production packet keeps that source connection visible.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: The equation progression records \mathbf{F}_\text{prop} = \nabla\times(\boldsymbol{\mu}\times\mathbf{B}) + \lambda_g\,\mathbf{L}\times\mathbf{B}_\text{TZP}; q = \frac{r B_\phi}{R B_\theta} > q_\text{crit}; \mathbf{B}_g = -(2G/c^2)\nabla\times\mathbf{J}; w = 2. These relations give the support page enough structure to show how the canonical formula arises from nearby source material rather than from a disconnected title alone.
dictionary_sections.lemma_support: The supporting lemma is source locality: the selected relation appears in Propulsion_Field_Vimana_EDITED.md, and the adjacent context connects it to the title, domain, and surrounding argument. The packet therefore treats the formula, its explanatory paragraph, and its neighboring equations as one evidence cluster.

dictionary_sections.interpretive_role: The Propulsion Field extends the registry beyond the first thousand IDs by giving the new band a cited mathematical object, a text-rich dictionary page, and a derivation path attached to the exact source file.
dictionary_sections.tzp_thesaurus:
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=1.6em,itemsep=6pt,topsep=4pt]
\item Canonical carrier: the selected formula that anchors the entry.
\item Source context: the chapter prose that explains why the formula belongs in the registry.
\item TRAWIN closure: the reading of the formula through time, rotation, action, wave, integration, and normalization.
\end{enumerate}

derivation_layer.source_latex:
% BEGIN TZP DERIVATION LAYER
\tzpsection{Derivation Layer}
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=1.6em,itemsep=7pt,topsep=4pt]
\item
\[
\mathbf{F}_\text{prop} = \nabla\times(\boldsymbol{\mu}\times\mathbf{B}) + \lambda_g\,\mathbf{L}\times\mathbf{B}_\text{TZP}
\]
This line is the 1 source equation retained from Propulsion\_Field\_Vimana\_EDITED.md for the The Propulsion Field packet.
\item
\[
q = \frac{r B_\phi}{R B_\theta} > q_\text{crit}
\]
This line is the 2 source equation retained from Propulsion\_Field\_Vimana\_EDITED.md for the The Propulsion Field packet.
\item
\[
\mathbf{B}_g = -(2G/c^2)\nabla\times\mathbf{J}
\]
This line is the 3 source equation retained from Propulsion\_Field\_Vimana\_EDITED.md for the The Propulsion Field packet.
\item
\[
w = 2
\]
This line is the 4 source equation retained from Propulsion\_Field\_Vimana\_EDITED.md for the The Propulsion Field packet.
\item
\[
\sigma = \rho v^2
\]
This line is the 5 source equation retained from Propulsion\_Field\_Vimana\_EDITED.md for the The Propulsion Field packet.
\end{enumerate}
% END TZP DERIVATION LAYER
